results.question: What year has Advanced Programming?
results.expected_answer: Advanced Programming (ARC6173) is listed under Year 2.
results.ui_answer: Advanced Programming () is listed under Year 2.

results.question: Show the full subjet outline for ARC6133 Advanced Programming.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARC6133 Title: Advanced Programming Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide undergraduates with a basic comprehension of the C++ programming language and the concepts of Object Oriented Programming (OOP). Assessment: - Test 1: 20% - Test 2: 20% - Assignment: 10% - Final exam: 50% Course Contents: - Object oriented and C++ fundamentals - Object classes - Dynamic memory allocation - Inheritance and polymorphism - Basic OO design using UML - Advanced C++ concepts and applications Course Learning Outcomes: - Create objects and classes with input/output using C++ code and UML class diagrams. - Construct relationship between classes and objects using C++ code and UML class diagram. - Evaluate the usage of function templates and class templates in a program. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Advanced Programming Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide undergraduates with a basic comprehension of the C++ programming language and the concepts of Object Oriented Programming (OOP). Assessment: - Test 1: 20% - Test 2: 20% - Assignment: 10% - Final exam: 50% Course Contents: - Object oriented and C++ fundamentals - Object classes - Dynamic memory allocation - Inheritance and polymorphism - Basic OO design using UML - Advanced C++ concepts and applications Course Learning Outcomes: - Create objects and classes with input/output using C++ code and UML class diagrams. - Construct relationship between classes and objects using C++ code and UML class diagram. - Evaluate the usage of function templates and class templates in a program. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARM6133 Linear Algebra and Numerical Methods.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARM6133 Title: Linear Algebra and Numerical Methods Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 48 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: This course introduces students to the mathematical concepts and analysis methods of linear algebra, numerical methods and optimization for solving practical problems in science and engineering. Assessment: - Test 1: 15% - Test 2: 10% - Quiz: 5% - Assignment: 10% - Final exam: 60% Course Contents: - Linear algebra - Numerical methods - Optimization Course Learning Outcomes: - Use various methods in linear algebra to solve systems of algebraic equations. - Apply numerical methods to solve mathematical problems including interpolation, differentiation, integration, root finding, ordinary differential equations and systems of linear equations. - Determine the optimal solutions to the optimization problems using appropriate methods. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Linear Algebra and Numerical Methods Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 48 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: This course introduces students to the mathematical concepts and analysis methods of linear algebra, numerical methods and optimization for solving practical problems in science and engineering. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARR6123 Robotics – Machine Design and Mechanisms.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARR6123 Title: Robotics – Machine Design and Mechanisms Aliases: Robotics - Machine Design and Mechanisms Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 44 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To develop the skills for designing linkage mechanisms and mechanical systems that contain the combination of motors, gear trains, linkages and other moving parts. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Test: 20% - Final exam: 40% Course Contents: - Introduction - Kinematics fundamentals - Linkage synthesis - Engineering materials - Screws, fasteners and nonpermanent joints - Bearings, couplings and lubrication - Flexible mechanical elements Course Learning Outcomes: - Describe the fundamental of linkage mechanisms. - Choose suitable engineering materials based on criteria such as strength, flexibility, machinability, cost and weight. - Evaluate suitable mechanical systems which contain the combination of motors, gear trains, linkages and other moving parts. - Build a mechanical system which contain the combination of motors, gear trains, linkages and other moving parts. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Robotics – Machine Design and Mechanisms Aliases: Robotics - Machine Design and Mechanisms Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 44 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To develop the skills for designing linkage mechanisms and mechanical systems that contain the combination of motors, gear trains, linkages and other moving parts. results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARL6153 Feedback Control.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARL6153 Title: Feedback Control Aliases: Feedback control Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce time domain, root locus and frequency domain methods for analysis and design of feedback control systems. Assessment: - Test: 15% - Assignment: 15% - Lab: 10% - Final exam: 60% Course Contents: - Introduction to control systems - System modelling - Time domain analysis of control systems - Root locus method - Frequency domain analysis - Compensation techniques Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply differential equations and transfer functions to the modelling of physical systems. - Analyse transient response, error and stability of control systems using time domain and root locus methods. - Analyse properties of control systems using frequency domain methods. - Design compensators for control systems using strategic thinking. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Feedback Control Aliases: Feedback control Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce time domain, root locus and frequency domain methods for analysis and design of feedback control systems. Assessment: - Test: 15% - Assignment: 15% - Lab: 10% - Final exam: 60% Course Contents: - Introduction to control systems - System modelling - Time domain analysis of control systems - Root locus method - Frequency domain analysis - Compensation techniques Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply differential equations and transfer functions to the modelling of physical systems. - Analyse transient response, error and stability of control systems using time domain and root locus methods. - Analyse properties of control systems using frequency domain methods. - Design compensators for control systems using strategic thinking. results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARL6123 Electrical Machines and Power Systems.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARL6123 Title: Electrical Machines and Power Systems Aliases: Electrical Machines and Power System Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce the basic concepts and working principles of electrical machines and transformers as well as electrical power systems and their protection methods. Assessment: - Test: 10% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Assessment: 20% - Final exam: 50% Course Contents: - Introduction to machinery principles - Three-phase circuits - Transformers - D.C. machines - AC machines (Induction Motors & Synchronous Motors) Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply the machinery principles and balanced three-phase power system circuit analysis on electrical machines and transformers. - Analyze the circuit characteristics and performance of transformers. - Analyze the circuit characteristics and performance of DC and AC machines. - Demonstrate the characteristics and performance of single-phase transformer and induction motor. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Normalized title to plural 'Systems'.
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Electrical Machines and Power Systems Aliases: Electrical Machines and Power System Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce the basic concepts and working principles of electrical machines and transformers as well as electrical power systems and their protection methods. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Normalized title to plural 'Systems'.

Assessment: - Test: 10% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Assessment: 20% - Final exam: 50% Course Contents: - Introduction to machinery principles - Three-phase circuits - Transformers - D.C. machines - AC machines (Induction Motors & Synchronous Motors) Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply the machinery principles and balanced three-phase power system circuit analysis on electrical machines and transformers. - Analyze the circuit characteristics and performance of transformers. - Analyze the circuit characteristics and performance of DC and AC machines. - Demonstrate the characteristics and performance of single-phase transformer and induction motor. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Normalized title to plural 'Systems'.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARP6110-P1 Project I.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARP6110-P1 Title: Project I Aliases: ARP6110, Project Category: core Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2028 Credit Hours: 5 Contact Hours: 35 hours Pre-Requisite: Completed 60 credit hours (excluding Art & Humanities subjects) Objective: To enable students to apply knowledge and skills gained from different courses through solving of real world problems; acquire technical skills through literature review, analysis, design, implementation, and testing/evaluation; and develop soft skills needed to carry out a real world project. Assessment: - Part 1 – General Effort, Project Design, Research Proposal: 25% - Part 1 – Presentation: 10% Course Contents: - Project planning, analysis and design - Research proposal - Presentation Course Learning Outcomes: - Develop the strategies for background study, analysis, design, implementation, and evaluation based on real-world problem. - Demonstrate communication skills in writing and presenting the project design and implementation. - Employ contemporary digital and numeracy tools to critically analyze problems in artificial intelligence related fields. - Demonstrate self-awareness in continuous lifelong-learning skills. - Apply integrated problem solving to propose a research paper/proposal for commercialization of the product. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Normalized from single ARP6110 code in structure.
